topic: Data-driven FL Studio template profile classifier
context: The user captured 12 additional standard FL Studio template profiles after the initial Electro profile and requested profile-driven recognition across product workflows.
observation: Compact template profiles contain enough mixer names, routes, channel routes, reserved placeholder ranges, control routes, roles, and tool-policy flags to classify and preserve standard template topology without hard-coded per-template logic.
result: The classifier now loads compact profiles, scores live readbacks against names/routes/channel routes/reserved ranges, annotates runtime track roles and tool policies, and returns compact candidate/ambiguity metadata for product workflows.

valid_ranges.control_route_level_match: Exact profile level when live level is available; missing live level is accepted as a conservative control-route match.

known_pitfalls: Do not claim exact template identity for structurally identical profiles; use candidate_templates and ambiguous=true.
Do not suppress generic diagnostics globally; suppression must come from a matched profile role/tool_policy.
Do not feed raw dumps into implementation context when compact validated profiles are available.
reproduction_steps: Run existing Mix Doctor and Project Doctor regression tests.
open_questions: Find an additional read-only discriminator if exact UI template names are required for Chillout/Chillout-Ambient, HipHop-Trap/Trap, or Funk/Rock.
Decide whether future live smoke tests should capture plugin-parameter fingerprints for ambiguous templates.
next_recommended_action: Keep future standard-template changes data-driven in knowledgebase/templates/profiles/ and extend runtime logic only when a new readback field is needed.
